title: quercus dentata subsp. koreana
intro: Quercus dentata subsp. koreana, also known as Korean deciduous oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree in the Fagaceae family, native to East Asia. The medicinal properties of Quercus dentata subsp. koreana lie in its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, treating gastrointestinal issues, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Quercus dentata subsp. koreana include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
